tasks.description: Add canonical `wrapWithEffect` helper in `effects/index.tsx`. Accepts a resolved `FC<EffectComponentProps>` and config object (effectName, durationInFrames, effectFrames, intensity, params, schema). Wraps content with `EffectErrorBoundary` and calls `validateAndCoerceParams`. Then refactor `wrapWithClipEffects` (lines 116-182) to call `wrapWithEffect` for each effect slot. Fix effectFrames for continuous effects: pass `effectFrames: durationInFrames` instead of omitting it. Remove the `console.log('[EffectLookup]...')` at line 111 (per-frame noise). Keep the `console.warn` at line 127.
tasks.executor_notes: Added canonical `wrapWithEffect` in `effects/index.tsx` and refactored `wrapWithClipEffects` to use it for continuous, entrance, and exit effects. Continuous effects now pass `effectFrames: durationInFrames`; the custom lookup `console.log` was removed; the missing-continuous `console.warn` remains. Verified with the effects module tests, hooks module tests, and `tsc --noEmit`.

tasks.description: Refactor `EffectLayerSequence.tsx` (lines 41-51) to use `wrapWithEffect` instead of inline effect construction. Pass `effectFrames: durationInFrames`. Remove direct imports of `EffectErrorBoundary` and `validateAndCoerceParams`. Keep `getEffectRegistry().getSchema(...)` call — pass result as `schema` config to `wrapWithEffect`.
tasks.executor_notes: Refactored `EffectLayerSequence.tsx` to call `wrapWithEffect` with the resolved continuous effect, `effectFrames: durationInFrames`, and the schema from `getEffectRegistry().getSchema(...)`. Direct `EffectErrorBoundary` and `validateAndCoerceParams` imports were removed. Verified with the video-editor effects/components/compositions test modules and `tsc --noEmit`.
commands_run: npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/effects/ src/tools/video-editor/components/ src/tools/video-editor/compositions/

tasks.description: Refactor `EffectCreatorPanel.tsx`: replace `wrapPreviewEffect` (lines 157-175) with a call to `wrapWithEffect`, using the already-resolved `EffectComponent`. When component is null, return `<PreviewRect>` fallback. Remove direct import of `EffectErrorBoundary`. Remove `PREVIEW_FPS` constant (line 74) and replace `timelineFps ?? PREVIEW_FPS` (line 198) with `timelineFps ?? 30`.
tasks.executor_notes: Refactored preview wrapping in `EffectCreatorPanel.tsx` to call `wrapWithEffect` with the already-resolved preview component, kept the `<PreviewRect>` fallback when no component is available, removed the direct `EffectErrorBoundary` import, removed `PREVIEW_FPS`, and switched the fallback FPS to `timelineFps ?? 30`. Verified with the video-editor effects/components/compositions test modules and `tsc --noEmit`.
commands_run: npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/effects/ src/tools/video-editor/components/ src/tools/video-editor/compositions/

tasks.description: Fix EffectLayerSequence timing bug. Wrap the effect in `<Sequence from={startFrame} durationInFrames={durationInFrames}>` so effect components see local frame 0 at their start. Counter-offset children with `<Sequence from={-startFrame}>` inside the effect's children to restore global frame context. Remove manual frame gating (`frame < startFrame || frame >= endFrame`) — the outer Sequence handles mount/unmount. Remove `useCurrentFrame` import. Keep the `!clip.continuous` early return. Resulting structure: `<Sequence from={startFrame} durationInFrames={durationInFrames}>{wrapWithEffect(<Sequence from={-startFrame}>{children}</Sequence>, Effect, config)}</Sequence>`.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated `EffectLayerSequence.tsx` to mount the effect inside `<Sequence from={startFrame} durationInFrames={durationInFrames}>` and to wrap children in `<Sequence from={-startFrame}>` before passing them through `wrapWithEffect`. Manual frame gating was removed and `useCurrentFrame` is no longer imported. Verified with the video-editor effects/components/compositions test modules and `tsc --noEmit`.
commands_run: npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/effects/ src/tools/video-editor/components/ src/tools/video-editor/compositions/

tasks.description: Fix async registration race in `DynamicEffectRegistry.ts`. In `registerAsync` (lines 53-60): after `await compileEffectAsync(code)`, check if `this.dynamic[normalized]` now has different code than what was compiled. If so, discard the stale result and return early. Also add `stale` boolean cleanup guard in `useEffectRegistry.ts`'s `useEffect` (lines 32-51): set `stale = true` in cleanup, check `if (stale) return` before each `await registry.registerAsync(...)` call inside the batch loop.
tasks.executor_notes: Added a post-await stale-result guard in `DynamicEffectRegistry.registerAsync` so slower compiles cannot overwrite newer code, and added a `stale` cleanup guard in `useEffectRegistry` with checks before each `registerAsync` await in the batch loops. Verified with the effects module tests, hooks module tests, and `tsc --noEmit`.

tasks.description: Update and add tests. Run existing tests first: `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/effects/`. Add a test for `wrapWithEffect`: verify it wraps content with EffectErrorBoundary and passes validated params; verify fallback rendering when effect throws. Add a test for `registerAsync` race condition: register 'v1' for a name, start slow registerAsync for 'v1-old', then registerAsync 'v2' that completes faster, verify 'v2' is not overwritten. Update any imports in existing tests that reference moved/removed exports. Then run: `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/effects/`, `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/`, and `npx tsc --noEmit`. Fix any failures and re-run until all pass.
tasks.executor_notes: Ran `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/effects/` first. Verified the existing worktree includes the required `wrapWithEffect` coverage for validated params and error-boundary fallback plus the async `registerAsync` race coverage. Final verification passed with the effects module, the full `src/tools/video-editor/` suite, and `npx tsc --noEmit`. A temporary race reproduction test was also run successfully and deleted.

watch_items: DEBT: video-editor-effect-preview — preview fps source not exposed via existing context. T3 removes PREVIEW_FPS and inlines 30; verify no other consumers import it.
DEBT: video-editor-effect-registry — replaceEffectRegistry() in render mutates module state during React render. Plan assumes this is intentional; do NOT change it.
DEBT: video-editor-effect-registry — O(n) re-renders during effect initialization (batching gap). Plan does NOT address this; do not make it worse.
DEBT: bulk-properties-panel — BulkClipPanel still imports CustomEffectEditor. This plan does NOT touch BulkClipPanel; verify no accidental import breakage.
DEBT: clip-param-schema-evolution — clips with stale params from previous schema version not auto-normalized. Not in scope but don't regress validateAndCoerceParams behavior.
Assumption: Remotion <Sequence from={negative}> works correctly to restore global frame context for nested children. This is a documented pattern but verify in tests.
Assumption: getSchema is O(1) lookup — no per-frame caching needed despite audit concern.
Assumption: Effect application order (continuous innermost → entrance → exit outermost) in wrapWithClipEffects is intentional. Preserve exactly.
Risk: T4 changes component tree structure for EffectLayerSequence — may affect Remotion rendering if assumptions about Sequence nesting are wrong.
Risk: T5 race condition fix uses code string comparison — if two different code strings compile to equivalent components, both will register (acceptable, not a bug).

sense_checks.question: Does the outer `<Sequence from={startFrame}>` provide local frame 0 to the effect? Does the inner `<Sequence from={-startFrame}>` restore global frame context for children? Is the manual frame gating removed? Is useCurrentFrame no longer imported?
sense_checks.executor_note: The outer `<Sequence from={startFrame}>` now provides the effect-local frame range, the inner `<Sequence from={-startFrame}>` restores global frame context for children, manual frame gating is removed, and `useCurrentFrame` is no longer imported.

sense_checks.question: After `await compileEffectAsync(code)`, does registerAsync check `this.dynamic[normalized]?.code !== code` before writing? Does the useEffectRegistry cleanup set `stale = true` and is `stale` checked before each registerAsync call?
sense_checks.executor_note: `registerAsync` now checks the current dynamic entry after `await compileEffectAsync(code)` and discards stale results when newer code is already present; `useEffectRegistry` sets `stale = true` on cleanup and checks it before each async registration call.

sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. `src/tools/video-editor/effects/` passed, the full `src/tools/video-editor/` suite passed, `npx tsc --noEmit` passed, and the current effect tests cover both `wrapWithEffect` success/fallback behavior and the async registration race. A temporary race reproduction test also passed before it was deleted.

meta_commentary:
Execution guidance:

1. **T1 is the foundation** — read `effects/index.tsx` carefully before editing. The helper signature must accept a resolved component (not a type string) so the preview path in T3 can use it. The lookup logic stays in `wrapWithClipEffects`; only the wrap-and-render part is extracted.

2. **T2 and T3 are independent** and can be done in parallel after T1. Both are small refactors replacing inline wrapping with calls to `wrapWithEffect`.

3. **T4 modifies EffectLayerSequence again** after T2 already changed it. The Sequence nesting pattern (`from={startFrame}` outer, `from={-startFrame}` inner) is critical — get the signs right. Import `Sequence` from `remotion` if not already imported.

4. **T5 is independent** of T1-T4. The key insight: after an async `await`, the registry state may have changed. Compare the `code` string that was compiled against whatever is currently in `this.dynamic[normalized]` — if different, a newer registration won. The stale boolean in useEffectRegistry is belt-and-suspenders.

5. **effectFrames contract**: The unified rule is — entrance/exit effects get their computed effectFrames from duration calculation; continuous effects get `effectFrames = durationInFrames`. This matches what EffectLayerSequence and preview already do. The fix is in `wrapWithClipEffects` which previously omitted it for continuous.

6. **Do not touch** `replaceEffectRegistry()` render-time mutation, CustomEffectEditor in BulkClipPanel, or the effect application order in wrapWithClipEffects. These are known debt items outside scope.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Add wrapWithEffect helper and refactor wrapWithClipEffects, fix effectFrames for continuous, remove per-frame console.log
Step 2: Refactor EffectLayerSequence to use wrapWithEffect
Step 3: Refactor makePreviewComposition/wrapPreviewEffect to use wrapWithEffect, remove PREVIEW_FPS
Step 4: Fix EffectLayerSequence timing bug with nested Sequences for frame offset
Step 5: Fix registerAsync race condition with post-await check + stale guard in useEffectRegistry
Step 6: Update and add tests for wrapWithEffect and registerAsync race condition
Step 7: Final validation — run effect tests, full video-editor tests, and tsc --noEmit

validation.completeness_notes: All 7 plan steps mapped to tasks. Steps 6 and 7 are combined into T6 since they are both test/validation work that should run sequentially in one task.
